report_name: New 'MiniPlasma' Windows Zero-Day Resurrects Patched Flaw for Full System Control

threat_actors.name: Chaotic Eclipse
threat_actors.description: A security researcher who publicly disclosed the 'MiniPlasma' zero-day vulnerability and a proof-of-concept exploit, citing frustrations with Microsoft's bug bounty program.
threat_actors.goals: Public disclosure of a zero-day vulnerability to prompt a vendor response.

attack_patterns.name: Exploitation for Privilege Escalation
attack_patterns.description: The MiniPlasma vulnerability allows a local attacker with low-level user access to exploit the cldflt.sys driver to escalate privileges to SYSTEM, gaining complete control over a Windows 11 machine.

malware.name: MiniPlasma PoC
malware.description: A proof-of-concept exploit for the MiniPlasma vulnerability that leverages a regression of CVE-2020-17103 to achieve local privilege escalation to SYSTEM on fully patched Windows 11 systems.

tools.name: Mimikatz
tools.description: Mentioned as a tool an attacker could use to steal credentials from memory after successfully escalating privileges to SYSTEM using the MiniPlasma exploit.

vulnerabilities.name: MiniPlasma
vulnerabilities.description: An unpatched zero-day local privilege escalation (LPE) vulnerability in the Windows Cloud Files Mini Filter Driver (cldflt.sys) on modern Windows 11 systems. It is a regression of CVE-2020-17103, allowing an attacker to gain SYSTEM privileges by creating arbitrary registry keys in a restricted hive.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2020-17103
vulnerabilities.description: A previously patched privilege escalation vulnerability in the Windows Cloud Files Mini Filter Driver. The new MiniPlasma zero-day is a regression of this flaw, and the original PoC for this CVE now works again on updated systems.

identities.name: Microsoft
identities.description: Vendor of the Windows operating systems affected by the MiniPlasma vulnerability.

indicators.name: Suspicious SYSTEM Process Creation by User Process
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious SYSTEM Process Creation by User Process
id: 2b9e1f0a-7c8d-4b6e-9f0a-1b2c3d4e5f6a
status: experimental
description: Detects a process running with SYSTEM integrity being created by a parent process that is not a known system process or running with lower integrity. This is a strong indicator of local privilege escalation.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/05/miniplasma-windows-0-day-enables-system.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-18
tags:
  - attack.t1068
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    IntegrityLevel: 'System'
  filter_legit_parents:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\System32\services.exe'
      - '\System32\wininit.exe'
      - '\System32\lsass.exe'
      - '\System32\smss.exe'
      - '\System32\csrss.exe'
      - '\System32\winlogon.exe'
      - '\System32\svchost.exe'
  condition: selection and not filter_legit_parents
indicators.description: Detects a process running as NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM being spawned by a non-system user process, a common pattern for local privilege escalation exploits like MiniPlasma.

indicators.name: Anomalous Write to .DEFAULT Registry Hive
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Anomalous Write to .DEFAULT Registry Hive
id: 9a8b7c6d-5e4f-3a2b-1c0d-9f8e7d6c5b4a
status: experimental
description: Detects write operations to the HKEY_USERS\.DEFAULT registry hive by processes not running as SYSTEM. This is a key indicator for the MiniPlasma exploit.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/05/miniplasma-windows-0-day-enables-system.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-18
tags:
  - attack.t1068
level: high
logsource:
  category: registry_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetObject|startswith: 'HKEY_USERS\.DEFAULT\'
    EventType: 'SetValue'
  filter_system:
    User: 'NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM'
  condition: selection and not filter_system
indicators.description: Detects unauthorized or anomalous write operations to the .DEFAULT user registry hive by low-privileged user accounts, which is the core mechanism of the MiniPlasma exploit.

indicators.name: Anomalous cldflt.sys Interaction
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Anomalous cldflt.sys Interaction
id: 5d6e7f8a-9b0c-1d2e-3f4a-5b6c7d8e9f0a
status: experimental
description: Monitors for anomalous interactions with the cldflt.sys driver from unexpected processes, which could indicate exploitation of the MiniPlasma vulnerability.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/05/miniplasma-windows-0-day-enables-system.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-18
tags:
  - attack.t1068
level: medium
logsource:
  category: image_load
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    ImageLoaded|endswith: '\cldflt.sys'
  filter_legit:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\svchost.exe'
      - '\explorer.exe'
  condition: selection and not filter_legit
indicators.description: Detects anomalous interactions with the cldflt.sys driver from unexpected processes, a potential indicator of MiniPlasma exploitation.

relationships.source_name: Suspicious SYSTEM Process Creation by User Process
